commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=273ed32b7e9c5d8586f18843d095e06a90b5af32)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `messages.responseUsage` config default, resolver, schema/type exports, reply-runner fallback, and helper tests for per-channel usage-footer defaults.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +28. Total +86 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking PR defect by source inspection: configure `messages.responseUsage`, run `/usage off` or patch `responseUsage: off`, and the existing code deletes the session field so the new reply-runner fallback re-enables the configured default.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer handling because proof must be refreshed for the current head and the remaining config/session-state semantics are merge-critical.

target.title: feat(messages): config-level default for responseUsage (persistent /usage footer)
target.body:
## What
Adds a config-level default for `responseUsage` (the per-reply usage footer mode `on|off|tokens|full`). Today the mode is **per-session** — sticky within a session, but every new session starts effectively `off` until a user runs `/usage`. There is no way to say "footer on by default" (optionally per channel). This adds that, as a single optional field.

```jsonc
"messages": {
  "responseUsage": "full"
  // or per-channel with a fallback:
  // "responseUsage": { "default": "off", "discord": "full", "telegram": "tokens" }
}
```

**Precedence (most specific wins):** session value → channel entry → `default` → `off`.
A session `/usage <mode>` still overrides the config default (unchanged).

## Change (small, additive)
- `messages.responseUsage` config field — `MessagesConfig` type + `MessagesSchema` (zod, strict).
- `resolveMessagesResponseUsageDefault(configured, channel)` helper in `thinking.shared.ts` (+ re-export).
- One extra `??` in the existing resolve chain in `agent-runner.ts` (channel = `replyToChannel`, already in scope):

```ts
const responseUsageRaw =
  activeSessionEntry?.responseUsage ??
  (sessionKey ? activeSessionStore?.[sessionKey]?.responseUsage : undefined) ??
  resolveMessagesResponseUsageDefault(cfg.messages?.responseUsage, replyToChannel);
```

## Backward compatibility
Fully additive. When `messages.responseUsage` is unset, the helper returns `undefined`, the `??` chain falls through unchanged, and `resolveResponseUsageMode(undefined)` yields `off` — **identical to today**. No migration.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Without this change a session reads the usage footer as `off` until someone runs `/usage`; there is no on-by-default. This PR adds `messages.responseUsage`, whose value seeds any session that has not set its own mode (precedence: session value -> channel entry -> `default` -> `off`).

**Real environment tested**: MarvinMBP, production OpenClaw `2026.6.1-beta.2` install at `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist`, run against the real `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the resolver out of the **shipped dist** (not source, not a stub) and exercised the precedence against the live config with no gateway, no chat and no `/usage`, so the config file is provably the only input:
```console
$ node -e 'import("/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/thinking-DFfceuVt.js").then(m=>{const fn=Object.values(m).find(v=>typeof v==="function"&&v.name==="resolveMessagesResponseUsageDefault");const cfg=require("/Users/admin/.openclaw/openclaw.json").messages.responseUsage;for(const ch of ["discord","telegram","whatsapp"])console.log(ch,fn(cfg,ch));console.log("unset",fn(undefined,"discord"));})'
```

**Evidence after fix**: Console output of that command against the live install and config (a footer screenshot is deliberately not used here: a footer in a chat is identical whether it came from this seed or a manual `/usage full`, so it cannot discriminate the behavior — the resolver run can):
```console
live messages.responseUsage = {"discord":"full","telegram":"full"}

  session value: <unset>  channel: discord   -> seeded mode: full
  session value: <unset>  channel: telegram  -> seeded mode: full
  session value: <unset>  channel: whatsapp  -> seeded mode: undefined

  config UNSET (field removed):            -> seeded mode: undefined (=> off, prior behavior)
```
(`openclaw --version` -> `OpenClaw 2026.6.1-beta.2 (4a7af99)`; `grep -rl resolveMessagesResponseUsageDefault /usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist` -> present in `agent-runner.runtime-*.js` and `thinking-*.js`, i.e. the resolver under test is the one the running gateway uses.)

**Observed result after fix**: For a session that set nothing of its own, the shipped resolver returns the configured mode (`full`) for the mapped channels, `undefined` (-> `off`) for an unmapped channel with no `default`, and `undefined` (-> `off`) when the field is absent. That is exactly the seeding precedence this PR claims, with the config as the sole variable. The footer render path itself is unchanged.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end render fan-out per surface (unchanged by this PR — it only changes which mode is resolved). Read-site display in `src/acp/translator.ts`/TUI is out of scope.

## Scope
- **In:** the per-reply footer render path + config field + schema + tests.
- **Out (non-goals):** any change to what `full`/`tokens` render, new `/usage` modes, or rendering work. This is *only* the default seed.

Read-site note: `src/acp/translator.ts` / TUI read `session.responseUsage` directly for display; folding them into the same precedence helper is a reasonable follow-up but kept out here to stay atomic.
target.author: marvinthebored

target.title: [security] fix(providers): validate sibling endpoint overrides
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a shared provider endpoint override validator for sibling usage providers.
- Restrict MiniMax and Alibaba endpoint overrides to their expected provider domains by default.
- Reject non-HTTPS schemes, embedded userinfo, and percent-encoded host delimiters before credentials are attached to follow-up requests.
- Fail closed when an explicit endpoint override is rejected, instead of silently falling back to the default credentialed provider endpoint.
- Preserve explicit custom-proxy/test-host compatibility through opt-in unsafe HTTPS-only override switches.
- Add focused regression coverage for rejected attacker hosts, encoded-delimiter bypasses, rejected `http://` overrides, rejected `user:pass@` URLs, accepted provider-owned HTTPS/bare host overrides, opt-in custom HTTPS hosts, opt-in rejection of `http://`/userinfo, and fail-closed fetcher behavior.
- Update MiniMax and Alibaba provider docs to make the provider-owned HTTPS-only default and unsafe custom-host opt-in explicit.

## Compatibility note
This intentionally treats explicit `http://...` endpoint overrides as invalid, even for otherwise provider-owned hosts. These settings carry cookies or API credentials into provider requests, so preserving insecure HTTP compatibility would keep a credential-disclosure footgun. Bare host overrides such as `platform.minimaxi.com` and provider HTTPS URLs remain supported.

Arbitrary proxy/test domains are rejected by default for these credentialed provider paths. If a caller intentionally routes MiniMax or Alibaba Coding Plan traffic through a custom HTTPS proxy/test host, they can opt in explicitly with:

- `MINIMAX_ALLOW_UNTRUSTED_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true`
- `ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_ALLOW_UNTRUSTED_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true`

The opt-in only relaxes the provider-owned-host allowlist. It still rejects `http://`, embedded userinfo, and encoded host-delimiter tricks so credentials are not sent over insecure or authority-confusing URLs.

## Validation
- `swift run EndpointProof` from a temporary local package depending on this checkout's `CodexBarCore` — passed. The proof exercised the real CodexBar settings/env reader and fetcher paths with redacted credentials/cookies:

```text
PASS MiniMax accepted provider HTTPS overrides: hosts=platform.minimaxi.com,platform.minimax.io
PASS Alibaba accepted provider HTTPS override: host=pre-cn-hangzhou.alibabacloud.com
PASS MiniMax rejected attacker override before network: MINIMAX_CODING_PLAN_URL
PASS MiniMax rejected http override before network: MINIMAX_CODING_PLAN_URL
PASS MiniMax rejected userinfo override before network: MINIMAX_CODING_PLAN_URL
PASS MiniMax rejected encoded-host override before network: MINIMAX_CODING_PLAN_URL
PASS Alibaba API rejected attacker override before network: ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_QUOTA_URL
PASS Alibaba API rejected http override before network: ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_QUOTA_URL
PASS Alibaba API rejected userinfo override before network: ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_QUOTA_URL
PASS Alibaba API rejected encoded-host override before network: ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_QUOTA_URL
Endpoint override real env/settings-path proof passed
```

- `swift run DiagnosticSmoke` from a temporary local package depending on this checkout's `CodexBarCore` — passed. The proof exercises `ProviderDiagnosticError.errorCategory(_:)` for `AlibabaCodingPlanUsageError.invalidEndpointOverride("ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_QUOTA_URL")` and verifies the invalid endpoint override remains a configuration issue instead of being text-classified as API/network:

```text
category=configuration
safeDescription=Configuration issue - check provider source and settings
PASS Alibaba invalid endpoint override diagnostic is configuration
```

- `swiftc -parse Sources/CodexBarCore/ProviderEndpointOverrideValidator.swift Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/MiniMax/MiniMaxSettingsReader.swift Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Alibaba/AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReader.swift Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/MiniMax/MiniMaxUsageFetcher.swift Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Alibaba/AlibabaCodingPlanUsageFetcher.swift Tests/CodexBarTests/MiniMaxProviderTests.swift Tests/CodexBarTests/AlibabaCodingPlanProviderTests.swift` — passed.
- Temporary standalone smoke executable using the real validator/settings-reader files — passed:

```text
endpoint override opt-in smoke: PASS
```

- `swift build --target CodexBarCore` — previously passed on this PR branch.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `make check` — SwiftFormat lint mode previously passed (`0/998 files require formatting`); SwiftLint was blocked by the existing local SourceKit issue (`Loading sourcekitdInProc.framework/Versions/A/sourcekitdInProc failed`).
- `swift test --filter 'MiniMaxProviderTests|AlibabaCodingPlanProviderTests'` — still blocked before the focused tests by the existing local `KeyboardShortcuts`/SwiftUI `#Preview` macro toolchain issue (`PreviewsMacros` plugin not found) with active developer directory set to CommandLineTools rather than full Xcode.
target.author: hinotoi-agent
